# services.ndppd

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.ndppd`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.ndppd.configFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to configuration file. |
| `services.ndppd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable daemon that proxies NDP (Neighbor Discovery Protocol) messages between interfaces. |
| `services.ndppd.interface` | `null or string` | Interface which is on link-level with router. (Legacy option, use services.ndppd.proxies.\<interface>.rules.\<network> instead) |
| `services.ndppd.network` | `null or string` | Network that we proxy. (Legacy option, use services.ndppd.proxies.\<interface>.rules.\<network> instead) |
| `services.ndppd.proxies` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | This sets up a listener, that will listen for any Neighbor Solicitation messages, and respond to them according to a set of rules. |
| `services.ndppd.proxies.<name>.interface` | `null or string` | Listen for any Neighbor Solicitation messages on this interface, and respond to them according to a set of rules. Defaults to the name of the attrset. |
| `services.ndppd.proxies.<name>.router` | `boolean` | Turns on or off the router flag for Neighbor Advertisement Messages. |
| `services.ndppd.proxies.<name>.rules` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | This is a rule that the target address is to match against. If no netmask is provided, /128 is assumed. You may have several rule sections, and the addresses may or may not overlap. |
| `services.ndppd.proxies.<name>.rules.<name>.interface` | `null or string` | Interface to use when method is iface. |
| `services.ndppd.proxies.<name>.rules.<name>.method` | `one of "static", "iface", "auto"` | static: Immediately answer any Neighbor Solicitation Messages (if they match the IP rule). iface: Forward the Neighbor Solicitation Message through the specified interface and only respond if a matching Neighbor Advertisement Message is received. auto: Same as iface, but instead of manually specifying the outgoing interface, check for a matching route in /proc/net/ipv6_route. |
| `services.ndppd.proxies.<name>.rules.<name>.network` | `null or string` | This is the target address is to match against. If no netmask is provided, /128 is assumed. The addresses of several rules may or may not overlap. Defaults to the name of the attrset. |
| `services.ndppd.proxies.<name>.timeout` | `signed integer` | Controls how long to wait for a Neighbor Advertisement Message before invalidating the entry, in milliseconds. |
| `services.ndppd.proxies.<name>.ttl` | `signed integer` | Controls how long a valid or invalid entry remains in the cache, in milliseconds. |
| `services.ndppd.routeTTL` | `signed integer` | This tells 'ndppd' how often to reload the route file /proc/net/ipv6_route, in milliseconds. |
